info.title: Identity API
info.description:
Authentication, OAuth 2.0, user management, roles, teams, departments, and multi-tenant account provisioning.

## Sections

- [Authentication](#authentication)
- [Contractors](#contractors)
- [Department Users](#department-users)
- [Departments](#departments)
- [Employees](#employees)
- [Oauthhandlers](#oauthhandlers)
- [Roles](#roles)
- [Team Users](#team-users)
- [Teams](#teams)
- [User Management](#user-management)
- [Users](#users)

post.description:
Upload or update user avatar

This is genuine business logic:
- File validation and type checking
- File storage and naming
- Image processing (could be extended)

delete.summary: Delete user avatar and clean up files
delete.description:
Delete user avatar and clean up files

This is genuine business logic:
- File system cleanup
- Cascade deletion logic

post.summary: Apple sends the OAuth response as POST (form-urlencoded) to the redirect URI
post.description:
Apple sends the OAuth response as POST (form-urlencoded) to the redirect URI.
Parse the body and continue as GET so Passport can complete the flow.

timestamp.description: Unix epoch milliseconds when the broker built the payload

signature.description: hex-encoded HMAC-SHA256 of `${timestamp}.${canonicalPayload}` using OAUTH_BROKER_SHARED_SECRET; canonicalPayload = provider,id,email,name,given_name,family_name,picture joined with newline (see AuthenticationService)
